teachings.teaching: Suffering has a purpose: through “the travail of his soul,” the servant reaches a result of fulfillment/satisfaction.
Knowledge and understanding are central—“by his knowledge” he is portrayed as achieving the desired outcome.
The righteous servant will “justify many,” meaning many people are made right through his role.
The servant bears others’ wrongdoing: he “will carry their iniquities.”

goals.goal: To teach that the suffering and burden carried by God’s servant brings redemption—bringing many into righteousness and bearing their sins/iniquities.
